steps.title: Use vertex form with the maximum point
steps.content:
The parabola reaches its maximum at (2.1, 70.56). For any parabola with vertex $(h_v, k_v)$, the vertex form is $h = a(t - h_v)^2 + k_v$. Here, the vertex is at $t = 2.1$ and $h = 70.56$, so:
$$h = a(t - 2.1)^2 + 70.56$$
The parabola opens downward (since the ball falls), meaning $a \lt 0$.

steps.title: Find the coefficient a using the ground impact
steps.content:
The ball hits the ground at $t = 4.2$ seconds, so $h = 0$ at that moment. Substitute into the equation:
$$0 = a(4.2 - 2.1)^2 + 70.56$$
$$0 = a(2.1)^2 + 70.56$$
$$0 = 4.41a + 70.56$$
$$a = \frac{-70.56}{4.41} = -16$$

steps.title: Write the final equation
steps.content:
Substituting $a = -16$ into the vertex form gives:
$$h = -16(t - 2.1)^2 + 70.56$$
That confirms D is correct.
